title: Constructing Holomorphic Functions on Universal Coverings of Complex Algebraic Varieties
speaker: Yohan Brunebarbe
abstract: Which complex analytic spaces can arise as the universal cover of a complex algebraic variety? Motivated by this question, Shafarevich asked whether the universal cover of a smooth projective variety X is always holomorphically convex — that is, whether there exists a proper holomorphic map from the universal cover of X to a Stein space. This was established in the linear case — when the fundamental group of X admits an almost faithful complex linear representation — by Eyssidieux–Katzarkov–Pantev–Ramachandran, using tools from non-abelian Hodge theory. In this talk, I will discuss a generalization of Shafarevich’s question to the case of non-compact algebraic varieties. This is joint work with Ben Bakker and Jacob Tsimerman.
tags: Workshop on Recent Developments in Hodge Theory and O-minimality

proof_stages.label: Shafarevich question and holomorphic convexity
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames universal covers of complex algebraic varieties and formulates Shafarevich's question by defining holomorphic convexity as the existence of a proper holomorphic map to a Stein analytic space, equivalently to a closed analytic subspace of some C^N in this setting.

proof_stages.label: Known linear theorem and failures of naive extensions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls the theorem that covers killing all rank-n complex local systems are holomorphically convex, notes that a faithful linear representation would imply Shafarevich's conjecture, and explains through obstructions that projectivity, normality, and restrictions on covers cannot simply be dropped.

proof_stages.label: Absolute nonextendable substacks as the correct setting
proof_stages.summary: The main joint result is stated for a normal complex algebraic variety and an absolute, nonextendable subset of the Betti moduli stack: under these hypotheses the associated covering is holomorphically convex.

proof_stages.label: Prototype proof for an integral polarized variation of Hodge structure
proof_stages.summary: In the special case where the representation is nonextendable and underlies an integral polarized variation of Hodge structure, the proof uses a period map, the equivalence between nonextendability and properness, and plurisubharmonic exhaustion functions to make the image Stein.

proof_stages.label: General Shafarevich morphism for a representation
proof_stages.summary: The first general step is to construct a unique algebraic fibration contracting exactly those algebraic subvarieties on which the representation becomes trivial, thereby giving a proper holomorphic map from the relevant cover to a weak Stein-like target with no positive-dimensional compact analytic subspaces.

proof_stages.label: Shafarevich saturation and Hodge-theoretic replacement
proof_stages.summary: An arbitrary representation is replaced by its Shafarevich saturation, an absolute Hodge substack with the same contracted subvarieties, whose structure supplies quasi-unipotent local systems, polarized variations of Hodge structure, and mixed-Hodge deformation data.

proof_stages.label: p-adic integrality maps and final fiber control
proof_stages.summary: Using p-adic integrality results and harmonic maps to buildings, the speaker combines all Hodge-theoretic and p-adic maps so that connected components of fibers are compact, allowing Stein factorization to produce the Shafarevich morphism and the desired holomorphic convexity.

examples.name: Simple abelian surface cover from an H^1 class
examples.note: For a simple abelian surface, the infinite cover associated to a nonzero H^1 class has no nonconstant holomorphic functions, showing that arbitrary infinite étale covers require an additional absoluteness-type restriction.
